text.title: Component Authoring Rules
text.summary: forwardRef, ARIA wiring, CSS variable theming, and API design rules for UI library primitives.

rule.statement: Wrap every DOM-emitting component in forwardRef. Set displayName explicitly.
rule.rationale: Without forwardRef, consumers cannot attach refs for focus management. Missing displayName produces "ForwardRef" in DevTools and stack traces.

rule.statement: Input components must generate a unique id via useId(), link label via htmlFor, and wire aria-invalid + aria-describedby for error and helper text.
rule.rationale: Screen readers cannot associate labels and error messages without explicit id linkage; useId() prevents SSR hydration mismatches.

rule.statement: Use CSS custom properties for all theme values (colors, radii, spacing). Never use ThemeContext or React-based theming inside the library.
rule.rationale: CSS variable theming is stack-agnostic; React Context theming locks consumers into the library's React version and context tree.

example.note: Importing a ThemeContext from the library into a styled.button — consumers must then wrap their entire app in the library's ThemeProvider.

rule.statement: Use class-variance-authority (cva) for variant props. Forbid long inline class strings and dynamic class interpolation.
rule.rationale: cva produces a typed, exhaustive variant map; inline conditional class strings grow uncontrollably and lose type safety.
